notes: Connector list sourced directly from the official JBL SRX800 Series User's Guide (PDF). The back panel diagram and descriptions on pages 7-9 confirm: 2 XLR/TRS combo inputs, 2 XLR direct outputs (DSP bypass), 1 XLR Mix Out (post-DSP), 1 EtherCon network port, and a locking IEC power inlet. The Thomann listing cited by the prior reviewer only showed a subset of ports. The EtherCon connector is for HiQnet peer-to-peer networking between SRX800 units only - not for connection to a standard LAN. This is a floor-standing/pole-mount powered PA speaker; rackUnits omitted.

ports.name: CH1 Input
ports.note: Balanced XLR female + 1/4" TRS combo; accepts mic, line, and instrument levels

ports.name: CH1 Direct Out
ports.note: Balanced XLR male; passes CH1 signal directly from input, bypassing DSP for zero latency; for daisy-chaining

ports.name: CH2 Input
ports.note: Balanced XLR female + 1/4" TRS combo; accepts mic, line, and instrument levels

ports.name: CH2 Direct Out
ports.note: Balanced XLR male; passes CH2 signal directly from input, bypassing DSP for zero latency; for daisy-chaining

ports.name: Mix Out
ports.note: Balanced XLR male; post-DSP mix of CH1 and CH2; adds approx. 2ms latency

ports.name: Network (EtherCon)
ports.note: HiQnet network control; for linking multiple SRX800 units; NOT to be connected to a LAN
